
# General Requirements

## E.G.1: macOS Target
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The application must target macOS (OS X) as the primary supported platform.

## E.G.2: Electron App Initialization
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Initialize a new Electron application with a working main process and renderer.

## E.G.3: Default Window
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


On launch, open a single main window with a default size and title.

## E.G.4: Local Dev Scripts
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Provide npm scripts to run the app in development mode on macOS.

## E.G.5: Minimal UI (Removed)
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

This requirement has been removed.

## E.G.6: Electron Version
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Use Electron v40.0.0 as the required version for this project.

## E.G.7: npm Version
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Use npm v11.8.0 as the required package manager version.

## E.G.8: Node.js Version
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Use Node.js v25.5.0 as the required runtime version for this project.

## E.G.9: Fullscreen on Launch
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

On launch, the main application window must open maximized, but not in fullscreen mode.

## E.G.10: App Name
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The application name must be "Clerkly".

## E.G.11: TypeScript Codebase
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


All application code must be written in TypeScript.

## E.G.12: UI Reference Source
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


A Figma-generated UI reference is stored in docs/development/ui_reference and must be used as the visual reference for the app interface.

## E.G.13: Local SQLite Storage
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Application data must be stored in a local SQLite database. If the database file is missing, the application must create it. If a database exists but its schema is not current, the application must run migrations to bring it to the latest schema. A backup of the existing database must be created before any migration is executed.

## E.G.14: Google Auth Gate Screen
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


If the application is not authorized with the user’s Google account, the app must show a single authorization screen and must not show the sidebar menu.

## E.G.15: Google Sign-In Button
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The authorization screen must include a “Sign in with Google” button.

## E.G.16: System Browser Login
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Clicking the “Sign in with Google” button must open the system browser for Google login.

## E.G.17: Post-Auth Success UI
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


After successful authorization, the app must return to the application and display the full interface.

## E.G.18: Auth Error Handling
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


If authorization fails, the app must show an error message and keep the Google sign-in button available.

## E.G.19: Module Boundary Separation
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The main process, preload script, and renderer must be separated into distinct modules. Cross-layer imports are prohibited; communication between layers must occur only via IPC.

## E.G.20: OAuth Redirect Mechanism
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Google OAuth must use a loopback redirect URI in the form "http://127.0.0.1:<port>/auth/callback" for desktop authorization.

## E.G.21: OAuth Client ID Source
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The Google OAuth client ID must be defined in src/auth/auth_google.ts as the single source of truth.

## E.G.22: Authorization Success Criteria
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Authorization is considered successful only when the OAuth callback includes a "code" parameter.

## E.G.23: UI Gate Policy
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The full application UI must be shown only after a successful OAuth callback; the authorization screen must remain visible otherwise.

## E.G.24: OAuth Error Handling
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


If OAuth returns an error or no code, the app must show an error message and keep the Google sign-in button available.

## E.G.25: Auth IPC Contract
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The IPC channels "auth:open-google" and "auth:result" are part of the stable auth contract and must not be changed without updating requirements.
